name: feat-ESE-12-3
title: WGSL codegen from kernel DSL definitions
description:
Implement the GpuKernelDef DSL and the generateWgsl() function that produces valid WGSL compute shaders from kernel definitions. The DSL declares query, read/write component access, uniforms, workgroup size, and a WGSL body snippet. The generator wraps the body with binding declarations, dispatch guard, and entry point.

Must handle intent components (GpuForce, GpuImpulse, GpuTeleport) as read bindings and physics-owned components (Transform, Velocity) as write bindings in the same kernel.

Inspired by bevy_gpu_compute's attributed struct pattern and bevy_hanabi's modifier code-fragment stitching. See engine/gpu/architecture.md sections 5 and 6.
acceptanceCriteria:
- GpuKernelDef interface: name, query, read[], write[], uniforms, workgroupSize, wgsl body
- generateWgsl() produces valid WGSL from a kernel definition
- TypedArray constructors map to WGSL types: Float32Array->f32, Int32Array->i32, Uint32Array->u32
- Binding layout: uniform struct, index buffer, read component fields, write component fields
- Generated WGSL includes dispatch guard (if id.x >= arrayLength)
- Component field names become WGSL variable names with namespace safety
- Handles intent+authority pattern: a physics kernel can read GpuForce/GpuImpulse/GpuTeleport and write Transform/Velocity in one kernel
- Unit tests: generate WGSL for movement kernel, particle kernel, and physics integration kernel (with intent components); validate output compiles
demoDeliverable: Define three GpuKernelDefs and show generated WGSL for each: (1) simple movement kernel (read Velocity, write Transform), (2) particle integrate kernel (read Velocity + GpuParticleLife, write Transform + GpuParticleLife + GpuParticleVisual, uniforms dt/gravity), (3) physics integration kernel (read GpuForce + GpuImpulse + GpuTeleport + GpuRigidBody, write Transform + Velocity, uniforms dt/substeps). Show all three compile via device.createShaderModule().
testingScenarios:
- Movement kernel generates correct binding count (1 uniform + 1 index + 3 read + 3 write = 8 bindings)
- Physics kernel includes intent component fields (fx,fy,fz,ix,iy,iz,tx,ty,tz,active) as read bindings
- Field name collision handling when two components share field names
- Empty uniforms: kernel with no uniforms omits uniform struct
- Tag components excluded from bindings but included in query
- Uint8Array maps to u32 in WGSL (for GpuTeleport.active)
- Generated WGSL compiles via device.createShaderModule() without validation errors
stakeholderUnderstanding: The kernel DSL is the developer-facing API — it lets system authors define GPU compute work declaratively. generateWgsl() turns component schemas into WGSL binding declarations and wraps a user-provided WGSL body snippet. The key value is that system authors never manually write binding boilerplate — they just declare which components they read/write and provide the math.
